assertions.title: Moving employee between departments updates both budgets

steps.action: Move 'MoveMe planning-budget-b' department Engineering -> Sales and inspect Budget panel
steps.expected: Engineering spend/headcount decrease and Sales increase without refresh
steps.observed: After move, employee card department changed to Sales and Budget panel reflected Engineering/Sales redistribution; undo/redo toggled the before/after budget states

assertions.title: Budget vs actual dashboard with utilization

steps.expected: Per-department envelope, committed spend, planned spend, remaining budget, utilization% columns
steps.observed: View showed budget-vs-actual chart and per-department spend/utilization cards (e.g., '$50,000 / $300,000', On track/Over budget) but no explicit committed-spend vs planned-spend vs remaining-budget column set

assertions.issues: Required per-department committed/planned/remaining column breakdown was not present in the rendered vs Actual UI.

steps.action: Open Budget -> Projection with planned future-start employee present
steps.expected: 12-month projection includes future-start impact in later months
steps.observed: Projection rendered with '12-Month Cost Projection'; current run-rate was $495,000 and year-end projected was $555,000, indicating planned future-start costs layered into forward months

assertions.title: Budget alerts at threshold and exceeded

steps.action: Set Marketing to a 90% state and inspect Budget panel
steps.observed: Marketing displayed '>80% used' with Used % = 90.0 and remaining $10,000

steps.action: Return Marketing to over-budget state and inspect Budget panel
steps.observed: Marketing displayed 'Over budget' with Used % = 110.0 and negative remaining budget

assertions.title: Approved request updates budget; rejected does not

steps.action: Create owner approval chain, submit 2 Engineering requests from invited submitter, approve one and reject one as owner
steps.expected: Approved request increases budget; rejected request leaves budget unchanged
steps.observed: Queue moved to Approved(1)/Rejected(1); total salary cost moved from $655,000 pre-actions to $705,000 post-actions (+$50,000), matching only the approved request amount while the rejected $40,000 request did not add extra budget impact

assertions.title: Employee deletion cascades manager references

steps.action: Delete manager 'Mgr planning-budget-b' (3 direct reports) and verify hierarchy + downstream references
steps.expected: Reports reassigned/top-level, deleted manager absent from manager selectors, Spreadsheet manager field updated
steps.observed: Hierarchy no longer showed the manager and reports were displayed as top-level, but post-delete API validation showed all three reports retained the deleted managerId reference; Spreadsheet view did not expose a manager column in this configuration, and Add Employee manager selector was unavailable because Add Employee is disabled when approval chains are active

evidence.network: DELETE /api/employees/69e29f1cbc5703b857f52e3a -> 200; follow-up employee fetch showed reports still carrying managerId 69e29f1cbc5703b857f52e3a

assertions.issues: Cascade was incomplete: report managerId references remained pointed at the deleted manager ID instead of being reassigned/cleared.

frictions.description: Approval queue action buttons for actionable rows are icon-only (no visible text/aria label), making automation mapping ambiguous.
frictions.resolution: Inspected DOM button classes in-session to map green=approve and red=reject before executing actions.

frictions.description: When approval chains are enabled, Add Employee is disabled in UI, limiting direct access to manager-selector validation paths.
frictions.resolution: Used alternate surfaces (Hierarchy/Spreadsheet) and API confirmation for cascade-state verification.

frictions.description: Manager delete control was not reliably accessible from inline-edit surfaces in this run.
frictions.resolution: Used authenticated in-session API delete for the target manager, then validated effects via UI views.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 4 passed (VAL-BUDGET-006/008/009/011) and 2 failed (VAL-BUDGET-007, VAL-CROSS-019).
